rulings.Case Title: PAUL MONTWILLO VS. WILLIAM TULL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Disqualify Defense Counsel David Wong
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, AUGUST 23, 2007, LINE 3. PLAINTIFF PAUL MONTWILLO'S Motion To Disqualify Defense Counsel David Wong: HEARING REQUIRED. THE RECORD APPEARS TO ESTABLISH THAT MR. WONG WAS COUNSEL FOR A.A.P. THIS DISPUTE CENTERS ON THE RIGHTS OF THE TWO PARTIES WHO FORMED AND OPERATED A.A.P. THE OPINIONS OF THE STATE BAR MAKE CLEAR THE OBLIGATION OF CORPORATE COUNSEL TO REPRESENT ONLY THE INTERESTS OF THE CORPORATION AND NOT THE INTERESTS OF ONE OWNER OVER ANOTHER. HOWEVER, EXHIBITS F THROUGH H OF THE WONG DECLARATION APPEAR TO ESTABLISH THAT THE CONFLICT HAS BEEN WAIVED. =(302/PJM/AP)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT AREVALO et al VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant City And County Of San Francisco, Department Of Public Works' Motion For Summary Judgment - (Recusal Of Judge Peter J. Busch.)
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, AUGUST 23, 2007, LINE 15. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S, DEPARTMENT OF PUBLIC WORKS, MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT: Motion for summary judgment re Derek Madaris' claims is granted. Plaintiff is unable to establish a prima facie case of race or national origin or gender discrimination or retaliation. Both white male and female employees were laid off as a part of the City's budget problems. Plaintiff was placed in an other job with the City as were the two female employees about whom plaintiff complains. Plaintiff contends that the females received a better job, but it was one without benefits and it was one that the females had prior experience doing. The decision to terminate plaintiffs was admittedly made by the head of the department, Ed Lee, and there is no evidence that his decision was based on race or sex or to retaliate; rather, it was based on the need to cut the budget. As a consequence, the derogatory comments of Montgomery and Monahan do not help Plaintiff establish a prima facie case. Plaintiff's loss of the library position admittedly was due to the end of a job he knew to be temporary. Assuming Plaintiff establishes a prima facie case, the City has presented a legitimate, non-discriminatory reason for its actions, a budget crises, and legitimate reasons for its decision to appoint Monahan and Anderson to the street inspector position and legitimate reasons for the termination of the library position. Plaintiff cannot demonstrate that the reasons presented were a pretext to remove Plaintiff from his job. As to Plaintiff's claim of retaliation, it is based on speculation. The Court has ruled on defendant's evidentiary objections. =(302/PJM)
